title: Import & Export Data
lede: Move your entire book of business into iMash in minutes. Upload a CSV of contacts, leads, or accounts, map the columns once, and let the built-in duplicate merging keep your CRM clean — then learn where to pull your data back out whenever you need it.

steps.title: Open the list you want to import into
steps.content:
Every CRM list — Contacts, Leads, and Accounts — has its own built-in CSV importer. You import directly into the list where you want the records to live.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM.
  2. In the sub-tab row, choose the list you want to add to: Contacts, Leads, or Accounts.
  3. Look at the toolbar above the table — the importer, duplicate finder, and API tools all live here.
💡 Not sure which list to use? Leads are prospects you are still qualifying, Contacts are people you actively do business with, and Accounts are the companies they belong to. The import steps are identical for all three.

images.alt: The CRM Contacts list with the import toolbar above the table
images.caption: CRM → Contacts — the toolbar above the table holds the Import, Find Duplicates, and API Info buttons

images.alt: The CRM Contacts list with the import toolbar above the table
images.caption: CRM → Contacts — the toolbar above the table holds the Import, Find Duplicates, and API Info buttons

steps.title: Prepare your CSV file
steps.content:
A minute of spreadsheet prep saves you from a messy import. Export your data from your old system (or Excel / Google Sheets) as a .csv file, and make sure:

  • Row 1 is a header row — column names like First Name, Email, Phone. iMash reads these to auto-match your columns.
  • One record per row — one person (or one company) per line.
  • Required columns are present:
    • Contacts & Leads: First Name (or Last Name) plus an Email or a Phone number.
    • Accounts: Name plus an Email or a Phone number.
  • Multiple values go in one cell, separated by commas — e.g. a Phone column can hold +15551230001, +15551230002 and a Tags column can hold vip, imported.
⚠️ Only .csv files are accepted — if you are in Excel or Google Sheets, use File → Save As / Download → CSV first. Rows that are completely empty are skipped automatically.

steps.title: Upload the file
steps.content:
1. In the list toolbar, click the Import button (the upload-arrow icon next to Add Contact / Add Lead / Add Account).
  2. The Import from CSV wizard opens on its upload step. Click inside the dashed area (or drag your file onto it) where it says Click or drag CSV file to this area to upload.
  3. Once the file is listed, click Next.
💡 If you see a CSV Parsing Error, the file is not a valid CSV — re-export it from your spreadsheet tool and try again.

images.alt: The list toolbar with the Import upload icon next to Add Contact
images.caption: The Import button is the upload-arrow icon in the toolbar, just left of Add Contact

images.alt: The list toolbar with the Import upload icon next to Add Contact
images.caption: The Import button is the upload-arrow icon in the toolbar, just left of Add Contact

images.alt: The Import Contacts from CSV upload step with a drag-and-drop area
images.caption: Step 1 of the wizard — drop your CSV into the dashed upload area

images.alt: The Import Contacts from CSV upload step with a drag-and-drop area
images.caption: Step 1 of the wizard — drop your CSV into the dashed upload area

steps.title: Map your columns to iMash fields
steps.content:
This is where the magic happens: each column from your file gets matched to an iMash field. iMash pre-fills its best guesses from your header names — you just review and adjust.

  1. For each CSV column on the left, pick the matching iMash field from the dropdown on the right — First Name *, Last Name *, Email, Phone Numbers, Lifecycle Stage, address fields, Tags, and more (Leads also get Source; Accounts get Name *, Website, and Industry).
  2. Pick Do not import for any column you want to skip.
  3. Pick Custom Variable (custom_vars JSONB) to keep extra data that has no standard field — a small Key box appears so you can name the variable (leave it blank to auto-generate a name from the header). Custom variables can later be used by your AI agents and templates.
  4. Columns mapped to Phone Numbers reveal two clean-up boxes: Trim leading digits (cut N characters off the front of every number) and Prefix to add (e.g. add +1). A live Preview underneath shows a sample number before → after, so you can normalize formats without touching your spreadsheet.
  5. (Contacts & Leads only) Use Select Account for Imported Contacts/Leads at the top to attach every imported record to one existing company account — handy when importing one client's staff list.
  6. Click Preview.
⚠️ If the Required Fields Missing warning appears, the Preview button stays disabled. Contacts/Leads need First Name (or Last Name) mapped together with Email or Phone Numbers; Accounts need Name plus Email or Phone Numbers.

images.alt: The column mapping step matching CSV headers to contact fields with phone transform options
images.caption: Each CSV column maps to an iMash field — phone columns get trim/prefix clean-up with a live preview

images.alt: The column mapping step matching CSV headers to contact fields with phone transform options
images.caption: Each CSV column maps to an iMash field — phone columns get trim/prefix clean-up with a live preview

steps.title: Preview, choose duplicate handling, and import
steps.content:
Before anything is written to your CRM, you get a final look at the data exactly as it will be imported.

  1. Review the sample table — it shows the first 5 rows with each column under the iMash field it will fill.
  2. Decide how duplicates should be handled with the Merge duplicate contacts checkbox:
    • Checked — if an incoming row matches an existing record, any new phone numbers are added to the existing record, and its email is filled in if it was empty. No duplicate rows are created.
    • Unchecked — duplicate rows are rejected and reported on the results screen, so nothing is silently overwritten.
  3. Confirm the Ready to Import banner shows the record count you expect, then click Import.
  4. A circular progress indicator shows records being processed in batches — large files (tens of thousands of rows) are handled automatically; just leave the window open until it finishes.

images.alt: The preview step showing sample rows, the merge duplicates checkbox and the Ready to Import banner
images.caption: The preview step — check Merge duplicate contacts to enrich existing records instead of rejecting them

images.alt: The preview step showing sample rows, the merge duplicates checkbox and the Ready to Import banner
images.caption: The preview step — check Merge duplicate contacts to enrich existing records instead of rejecting them

steps.title: Review the import results
steps.content:
When the import finishes, the wizard shows a scorecard:

  • Total Records — how many rows were processed.
  • Successful — records created (or merged) in your CRM.
  • Failed — rows that could not be imported.

If anything failed, an Error Details table lists each problem row with the reason. Rows rejected as duplicates can be expanded to see the existing record and the incoming record side by side, so you can decide what to fix.

  1. Click Close — your new records are already live in the list, ready to call, message, and automate.

images.alt: The import results screen with Total Records, Successful and Failed tiles
images.caption: The results scorecard — failed rows are listed with the exact reason

images.alt: The import results screen with Total Records, Successful and Failed tiles
images.caption: The results scorecard — failed rows are listed with the exact reason

steps.title: Clean up duplicates any time
steps.content:
Already imported from several sources? The Contacts and Leads lists have a built-in de-duplicator.

  1. In the list toolbar, click the Find Duplicates button (the copy icon).
  2. Choose a Duplicate scope — search across all records, or narrow the match rule.
  3. Under Find duplicates by, click Phone Number or Email Address to scan.
  4. Review each duplicate group and merge or remove the extras.
💡 Run this after every big import — clean data means your campaigns never call the same person twice.

images.alt: The Find and Remove Duplicate Contacts dialog with scope and match-by options
images.caption: Find & Remove Duplicate Contacts — scan by phone number or email address

images.alt: The Find and Remove Duplicate Contacts dialog with scope and match-by options
images.caption: Find & Remove Duplicate Contacts — scan by phone number or email address

steps.title: No list yet? Build one with People Explorer
steps.content:
You don't even need a spreadsheet to fill your CRM. The People Explorer in the Marketing section searches a huge directory of professionals and companies so you can build a targeted list from scratch:

  • Filter by job title, company, industry, location, and more.
  • Preview the matches, hand-pick the people you want, and inspect any record before importing.
  • Import them as Leads, Contacts, or Accounts — with the option to auto-create company accounts and link each person to theirs.

See the full walkthrough in Import People (People Explorer).

steps.title: Export your data
steps.content:
Your data is never locked in. The richest exports live where the activity happens:

  • Call history — go to Call Logs (in the Call Center section of the sidebar) and click Export to CSV (Max 50,000) to download your filtered call history. Click Columns first to choose exactly which columns appear on screen and in the CSV. In summary view the button becomes Export Summary, with Summary as CSV or Summary as PDF options.
  • Campaign results — open a campaign and use its Export buttons to download the call outcomes as CSV or PDF.
  • Reports — the Analytics reports pages offer CSV downloads of their tables.
  • CRM records via API — on any Contacts, Leads, or Accounts list, click the API Info button (the link icon in the toolbar) to get ready-made API examples for reading your records programmatically — perfect for syncing to another system. See API Keys to set up access.
💡 Exports respect the filters you have applied — narrow the list first (date range, agent, tags) and the file will contain only what you see.

images.alt: The Call Logs header buttons: Columns, Export to CSV (Max 50,000) and API Info
images.caption: Call Logs → Export to CSV — the Columns button controls which columns land in the file

images.alt: The Call Logs header buttons: Columns, Export to CSV (Max 50,000) and API Info
images.caption: Call Logs → Export to CSV — the Columns button controls which columns land in the file

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Import & Export Data - CRM | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Bulk-import contacts, leads, and accounts into iMash from CSV: column mapping, phone number clean-up, custom variables, and duplicate merging. Plus where to export call logs, campaign results, and CRM data.
